data.title: Mokingran Jewellery Sets 2025 AGM to Approve Profit Plan, Financing and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2585) ).

Mokingran Jewellery Group has called its 2025 annual general meeting for May 8, 2026 in Shandong Province, where shareholders will review the board’s work report, the 2025 annual report and a proposed profit distribution plan. The agenda also covers approval of 2026 composite credit facilities, a guarantee plan, an annual cap for futures and derivatives trading, and the reappointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor.

Shareholders will additionally vote on a special resolution granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, including through convertible securities, within a defined period and subject to regulatory approvals. The mandate, if passed, would give Mokingran added flexibility for potential financing and capital management initiatives, which could influence its funding structure and future growth options.

More about Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H

Mokingran Jewellery Group Co., Ltd. is a China-based jewellery company listed in Hong Kong, focusing on the design, manufacture and sale of gold and jewellery products. The group serves the domestic consumer market from its base in Shandong Province and operates within the broader precious metals and retail jewellery industry.

Average Trading Volume: 529,460

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$5.76B

data.title: PAX Global Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Accounts, Board Slate and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

PAX Global Technology ( (HK:0327) ) just unveiled an update.

PAX Global Technology has convened its 2026 annual general meeting for 20 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will vote on adopting the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and re-electing seven directors to the board. The agenda also includes authorising the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re-appointing PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and granting directors a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, a move that preserves financial flexibility for potential capital raising and strategic initiatives, while reinforcing existing governance and leadership structures.

The proposed share issuance mandate at PAX Global Technology’s 2026 AGM would allow the board to allot new shares and resell treasury shares within defined limits, excluding rights issues and certain employee or scrip arrangements. This authority, renewed on an annual basis, is standard among Hong Kong-listed companies and is seen as maintaining the company’s ability to respond quickly to market opportunities without immediate recourse to a separate shareholder vote, which can be important for funding expansion, investments or balance sheet management.

More about PAX Global Technology

PAX Global Technology Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company in the electronic payments technology sector, providing payment terminal solutions and related services to merchants and financial institutions worldwide. The group focuses on secure, innovative transaction devices and platforms that support the growing global shift toward cashless and digital payments.

data.title: Guoquan Food Controlling Shareholder Cuts Stake, Group Agrees to 180-Day Lock-Up
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Company., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2517) ) has provided an announcement.

Controlling shareholder Shanghai Guoxiaoquan Enterprise Management Center agreed to sell 124,500,000 Guoquan Food shares, or about 4.74% of the company’s issued share capital, via a block trade at HK$3.80 per share, cutting its stake from 12.61% to 7.88%. Despite the sale, the concert-party group of controlling shareholders will still control around 44.66% of voting rights, and the company expects no material operational impact while highlighting that the transaction should increase free float and improve share liquidity.

Following the disposal, the controlling shareholders, including Mr. Yang, Mr. Meng, Mr. Li, Guoquan Industry, Guoxiaoquan EM and Guoxiaoquan Tech, have voluntarily committed to a 180-day lock-up on any further share disposals from April 16 to October 12, 2026. This lock-up is presented as a signal of their confidence in Guoquan Food’s long-term value and development prospects, though the company has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care when trading its securities.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2517) stock is a Buy with a HK$4.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Company., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2517 Stock Forecast page.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:2585) ) has shared an announcement.

Mokingran Jewellery Group has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.3 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 8 May 2026. The dividend for Hong Kong H-share investors will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 25 June 2026, with the ex-dividend date set for 12 May, a record date of 19 May and a book closure period from 14 to 19 May.

The company has detailed the withholding tax treatment for non-resident enterprise and individual H-shareholders, generally applying a 10% enterprise income tax and individual income tax rates of 10% or 20% depending on the relevant tax treaties with the PRC. This clarification provides greater visibility on net dividend receipts for overseas investors and underscores the company’s intent to return cash to shareholders while complying with cross-border tax requirements.

More about Mokingran Jewellery Group Co Ltd Class H

Mokingran Jewellery Group Co., Ltd. operates in the jewellery industry and is listed in Hong Kong via its H shares. The company focuses on the design, manufacturing and sale of jewellery products, serving investors in both mainland China and international markets through its Hong Kong listing structure.

data.title: Hin Sang Group suspends chairman and CEO after criminal custody in China
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hin Sang Group (International) Holding Co. Ltd. ( (HK:6893) ) has shared an update.

Hin Sang Group (International) Holding announced that its chairman, chief executive officer and executive director, Mr. Pang Siu Hin, has been placed in criminal custody by mainland China’s Public Security Bureau, in a case the board says does not involve the group’s operations or financial affairs. The board does not expect a material adverse impact on the company’s business or financial position but has cautioned shareholders and investors when dealing in its securities.

In response to Mr. Pang’s inability to perform his duties, the board has immediately suspended all his administrative and executive powers as chairman, CEO and executive director, citing the best interests of shareholders. Executive director Ms. Kwan Lai Man has been appointed Acting Chairperson, while company secretary Mr. Chen Yeung Tak replaces Mr. Pang as the company’s authorised representative and process agent with effect from 15 April 2026.

More about Hin Sang Group (International) Holding Co. Ltd.

Hin Sang Group (International) Holding Co. Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through its subsidiaries as part of a diversified group. The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and is subject to Hong Kong’s listing and securities regulatory framework.

data.title: Wai Chun Group Partners with Xavvi to Launch AI-Driven Influencer Commerce Platform
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Wai Chun Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1013) ) is now available.

Wai Chun Group Holdings has entered into a strategic cooperation with U.S.-based Xavvi US Inc to establish a holding platform company focused on AI-driven creator economy and influencer traffic monetisation. The partnership will build a global influencer matrix of more than 30,000 personalities, including celebrities, athletes and supermodels, and integrate these resources with Wai Chun’s listed-company platform.

The new platform will operate under a “One Star, One Store” model, creating independent digital brand stores for each influencer and using a Factory-to-Consumer structure to bypass intermediaries and support live-streaming e-commerce and brand endorsement. Revenue will primarily come from a share of gross merchandise value collected centrally by the platform, which targets a high gross profit margin of about 40%, signalling a potential shift in Wai Chun’s earnings profile and its positioning within the fast-growing AI-enabled influencer commerce segment.

More about Wai Chun Group Holdings Limited

Wai Chun Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through various subsidiaries and is currently pursuing business transformation toward internet technology and AI-driven creator-economy ventures. The group is positioning itself to tap into influencer-based e-commerce and traffic monetisation by partnering with emerging technology operators and leveraging digital platforms for new profit drivers.

data.title: Kangqiao Service Seeks PRC Supreme Court Retrial Over RMB473.7 Million Subsidiary Judgment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kangqiao Service Group Limited ( (HK:2205) ) has provided an announcement.

Kangqiao Service Group Limited has updated investors on ongoing litigation involving its wholly owned mainland subsidiary after Chinese courts previously held the unit liable for RMB473.7 million under loan guarantees linked to Henan Chengqiao. Controlling shareholder and chairman Song Gewei has undertaken to indemnify the listed company for any losses arising from this judgment, aiming to ring‑fence the financial impact at the shareholder level.

The company has applied to the Supreme People’s Court of China for a retrial, arguing that the appellate court misapplied PRC company law, misjudged the relationship between Henan Chengqiao and the subsidiary, and erred in finding the bank to be a good‑faith counterparty. It has also submitted new evidence challenging the validity of the underlying loan agreements, while noting that about RMB212 million of the subsidiary’s bank balances are under judicial enforcement but asserting that the issue remains confined to this unit and does not affect the group’s other operations.

More about Kangqiao Service Group Limited

Kangqiao Service Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property services provider incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating in mainland China through subsidiaries such as Kangqiao Yueshenghuo Service Group Co., Ltd. The group focuses on community and property management services, with its operations organized across multiple operating entities within the wider Kangqiao Service Group.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Keep, Inc. ( (HK:3650) ) has issued an update.

Keep Inc. has granted 660,000 restricted share units (RSUs), equivalent to about 0.13% of its issued share capital, to eight grantees under its post-IPO share incentive plan, with no purchase price and a reference share price of HK$2.87 on the grant date. The RSUs carry multi-year vesting schedules, performance-based vesting conditions, and clawback mechanisms tied to conduct and compliance, underscoring the company’s emphasis on long-term performance alignment and governance in its employee incentive structure.

The grants are split between two vesting structures, with one group vesting over four annual tranches and another vesting 50% after two years and the remainder over the following two years, all subject to minimum 12-month vesting. By tying vesting to individual performance evaluations and reserving the right to claw back awards in cases such as misconduct or breach of company policies, Keep aims to reinforce accountability, support retention of key personnel, and strengthen its competitive positioning in the fitness technology industry.

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited ( (HK:0984) ) has issued an update.

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) has entered into a three-year warehouse lease with Omaha Investments Limited, securing approximately 103,524 square feet at Hutchison Logistics Centre in Kwai Chung from 15 April 2026 to 14 April 2029. Under HKFRS 16, the lease will be recognized as a right-of-use asset of about HK$42.36 million, classifying the deal as a major transaction that supports the group’s logistics capacity and operational scale.

As no shareholder has a material interest in the deal, AEON Co., holding about 59.91% of the company’s shares, has provided written approval in lieu of a general meeting. The company will issue a circular to shareholders for information, with the transaction expected to streamline compliance while reinforcing AEON Stores’ infrastructure for inventory management and distribution in Hong Kong.

More about AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited operates retail and department stores in Hong Kong, focusing on general merchandise and consumer goods. The company serves a broad base of local shoppers and positions itself as a key player in the territory’s organized retail sector, leveraging scale and logistics to support its store network and customer offerings.

The latest update is out from China Rare Earth Holdings ( (HK:0769) ).

China Rare Earth Holdings said it has received additional resumption guidance from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, requiring it to re-comply with listing rules related to board composition, audit committee and risk management functions. The company remains responsible for devising an action plan to address the substantive issues behind the suspension of its shares and to fully meet all regulatory requirements before trading can resume.

Trading in China Rare Earth’s shares has been suspended since 18 June 2025 and will continue until the exchange is satisfied that all resumption guidance has been met and the issues leading to the halt have been remedied. The company’s board currently comprises two executive directors, one non-executive director and one independent non-executive director, and shareholders and potential investors are urged to exercise caution in dealing with the company’s securities.

More about China Rare Earth Holdings

China Rare Earth Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged through its subsidiaries in the rare earth sector, with its shares traded on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group operates under the Hong Kong regulatory and listing framework, and its governance structure includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors on the board.
